```text
1 # Handoff: WordSpell saved config has selectedUnits L4 but source.filter L1 — needs configMode wiring
2 
3 **Date:** 2026-04-29
4 **Branch:** feat/issue-216
5 **Worktree:** `worktrees/feat-issue-216`
6 **Worktree path:** `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/feat-issue-216`
7 **Git status:** clean (last commit `01fb3cd0` — soft-c fix)
8 **Last commit:** `01fb3cd0` fix(word-spell): use graphemesRequired (pair-based) so soft-c doesn't pull L1 s-words
9 **PR:** #219 — open — <https://github.com/leocaseiro/base-skill/pull/219>
10 
11 ## Resume command
12 
13 ```bash
14 /resync
15 cd /Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/feat-issue-216
16 yarn dev
17 # Then: write a failing test that reproduces the saved-config drift,
18 # then fix the root cause (option A or B below), commit, push.
19 ```
20 
21 ## Current state
22 
23 **Task:** Close out PR #219. The cumulative-Y filter, audit invariant, soft-c fix, levels.ts reconcile, and triStateForLevel cleanup are all merged into `feat/issue-216` and tests pass (1566/1566).
24 
25 **Open bug:** During manual smoke-test on the deployed PR preview, words like `it`, `an`, `pits`, `spits` (pure L1) appear in WordSpell rounds even after the user has selected only Level 4 chips in the picker.
26 
27 **Phase:** debugging a save-side bug. Soft-c collision was a red herring for the symptom the user observed; the soft-c fix is correct but doesn't cover this case.
28 
29 ## What I diagnosed
30 
31 The IDB dump at `scripts/dev/dump-2026-04-29_14h16.json` (on master) is the smoking gun. The `__last_session__` saved config has:
32 
33 ```jsonc
34 "config": {
35   // ...
36   "selectedUnits": [
37     { "g": "sh", "p": "ʃ" }, { "g": "ch", "p": "tʃ" }, { "g": "th", "p": "θ" },
38     { "g": "th", "p": "ð" }, { "g": "qu", "p": "kw" }, { "g": "ng", "p": "ŋ" },
39     { "g": "wh", "p": "w" }, { "g": "ph", "p": "f" }, { "g": "g", "p": "dʒ" },
40     { "g": "c", "p": "s" },
41     { "g": "e", "p": "" }, { "g": "f", "p": "" }, { "g": "l", "p": "" }
42   ],
43   "source": {
44     "filter": {
45       "graphemesAllowed": [s, a, t, p, i, n],         // ← L1 only
46       "phonemesRequired": ["s","æ","t","p","ɪ","n"]   // ← L1 only, pre-fix shape
47     }
48   }
49   // NO `configMode: 'simple'`
50 }
51 ```
52 
53 `selectedUnits` is correct (L4 + silent letters). `source.filter` is the L1 default from `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` and was never recomputed when `selectedUnits` changed. The game pulls rounds from `source.filter`, so it sees L1 graphemes only and emits L1 words.
54 
55 ## Root cause
56 
57 `resolveWordSpellConfig` in `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:210-277` only re-derives `source.filter` from `selectedUnits` when the saved config has `configMode: 'simple'`. The `__last_session__` config does NOT have `configMode` set, so it falls into the **advanced** branch (line 246-261), which spreads the saved config as-is over `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` — keeping the stale `source.filter` and the up-to-date `selectedUnits` side by side.
58 
59 Two upstream contributors:
60 
61 1. **`DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG`** at `$gameId.tsx:98-117` does not set `configMode: 'simple'` and uses the pre-fix `phonemesRequired` filter shape. The default config is what the user starts with on a fresh load.
62 2. **`onConfigChange={(c) => setCfg(resolveWordSpellConfig(c))}`** at `$gameId.tsx:465` calls `resolveWordSpellConfig` on every picker change, but `c` already has no `configMode`, so it stays in the advanced branch forever.
63 
64 `usePersistLastGameConfig` (the IDB writer at `src/db/hooks/usePersistLastGameConfig.ts`) writes whatever `cfg` is — it isn't the bug source.
65 
66 ## Decisions made
67 
68 - **`configMode: 'simple'` is the marker.** Setting it on the default config + on any picker-driven path will route the resolver through `resolveSimpleConfig`, which recomputes `source.filter` from `selectedUnits` and emits the post-fix `graphemesRequired` shape.
69 - **Don't touch `usePersistLastGameConfig`** — the persistence layer is correct; we just need to feed it a fresh resolution.
70 - **Keep TDD discipline (CLAUDE.md):** start with a failing test that asserts a saved config with `selectedUnits=L4 + source.filter=L1` resolves to a config with `source.filter=L4 cumulative + graphemesRequired=L4 pairs` after `resolveWordSpellConfig`.
71 
72 ## Spec / Plan
73 
74 - Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-wordspell-cumulative-y-filter-design.md`
75 - Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-wordspell-cumulative-y-filter.md` (Tasks 11–13 still mention manual VR + smoke; merge this fix before checking those off)
76 
77 ## Key files
78 
79 - `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:98` — `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` (needs `configMode: 'simple'` and the post-fix filter shape)
80 - `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:210` — `resolveWordSpellConfig` (the dispatch that picks simple vs advanced)
81 - `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:232` — the simple-mode branch that calls `resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig`
82 - `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:246` — the advanced branch that does the spread merge (the bug surfaces here)
83 - `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:465` — `onConfigChange` in InstructionsOverlay
84 - `src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.ts:37` — `resolveSimpleConfig` (already correct after `01fb3cd0`)
85 - `src/db/hooks/usePersistLastGameConfig.ts` — the IDB writer (correct, no changes needed)
86 - `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts` — existing test for `resolveWordSpellConfig`. Add the regression test here.
87 
88 ## Next steps
89 
90 ### Step 1 — Reproduce with a failing test (TDD red)
91 
92 In `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts`, add:
93 
94 ```ts

135   expect(required.some((u) => u.g === 'sh' && u.p === 'ʃ')).toBe(true);
136   expect(required.some((u) => u.g === 'c' && u.p === 's')).toBe(true);

147 Run `yarn vitest run src/routes/$locale/_app/game/resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts` — it MUST fail.
148 
149 ### Step 2 — Fix (TDD green)
150 
151 Two viable fixes; pick A unless something obvious blocks it:
152 
153 **A. Make `selectedUnits` an automatic simple-mode signal.** In `resolveWordSpellConfig` (`$gameId.tsx:210`), change line 232:
154 
155 ```ts
156 // before
157 if (saved.configMode === 'simple') {
158 
159 // after
160 if (saved.configMode === 'simple' || Array.isArray(saved.selectedUnits)) {
161 ```
162 
163 Rationale: any saved config that has `selectedUnits` is conceptually a simple-mode config — the picker produced it. Routing through `resolveSimpleConfig` recomputes `source.filter` from `selectedUnits` and emits the post-fix `graphemesRequired` shape every time.
164 
165 **B. Set `configMode: 'simple'` on `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG`** and on the resolver's advanced merge so future saves carry the marker. Less robust because in-flight saved configs without the marker still drift.
166 
167 Either way, also update `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` (`$gameId.tsx:98`) to use the post-fix shape:
168 
169 ```ts
170 // before
171 filter: {
172   region: 'aus',
173   graphemesAllowed: cumulativeGraphemes(1),
174   phonemesRequired: defaultSelection().map((u) => u.p),
175 },
176 
177 // after
178 filter: {
179   region: 'aus',
180   graphemesAllowed: cumulativeGraphemes(1),
181   graphemesRequired: defaultSelection().map((u) => ({ g: u.g, p: u.p })),
182 },
183 ```
184 
185 This kills the last `phonemesRequired` user in production code.
186

193 - Hard-test on the PR preview after the next push lands and CI rebuilds (~10 min): clear site data + reload, repeat the manual flow.

195 ### Step 4 — Commit
196 
197 ```bash

199         src/routes/\$locale/_app/game/resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts
200 git commit -m "fix(word-spell): re-derive source.filter from selectedUnits on every resolve
201 
202 Saved __last_session__ configs lacked configMode='simple', so
203 resolveWordSpellConfig fell into the advanced branch and never
204 re-derived source.filter from selectedUnits. Result: picker showed
205 L4 chips selected, but the persisted source.filter stayed at the
206 L1 default and the game produced L1 words.
207 
208 Treat any config with selectedUnits as simple-mode and route through
209 resolveSimpleConfig so source.filter always reflects the picker.
210 Also migrate DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG to the post-fix graphemesRequired
211 shape — last phonemesRequired user removed from production code.
212 
213 Regression test in resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts."
214 ```

119 const DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG: WordSpellConfig = {
120   gameId: 'word-spell',
121   component: 'WordSpell',
122   inputMethod: 'drag',
123   wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-auto-eject',
124   tileBankMode: 'exact',

132 
133 /**
134  * Deterministic default round values for NumberMatch. Using a fixed sequence
135  * (rather than `Math.random`) keeps VR baselines stable across runs — nanoid
136  * seeds in the route loader bypass any Math.random pin, so the only reliable
137  * fix is to remove randomness from the default config entirely. Users still
138  * customise via the settings panel.
139  */
140 const DEFAULT_NUMBER_MATCH_ROUND_VALUES: readonly number[] = [5, 8, 3];
141 
142 const makeDefaultNumberMatchConfig = (): NumberMatchConfig => ({
143   gameId: 'number-match',
144   component: 'NumberMatch',
145   inputMethod: 'drag',
146   wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-auto-eject',
147   tileBankMode: 'distractors',
148   distractorCount: 2,
149   totalRounds: 3,
150   // Deterministic order by default — see DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG comment.
151   roundsInOrder: true,
152   ttsEnabled: true,
153   mode: 'numeral-to-group',
154   tileStyle: 'dots',
155   range: { min: 1, max: 12 },
156   rounds: DEFAULT_NUMBER_MATCH_ROUND_VALUES.map((value) => ({ value })),
157 });
158 
159 export const resizeNumberMatchRounds = (
160   prev: NumberMatchRound[],
161   count: number,
162   range: { min: number; max: number },
163 ): NumberMatchRound[] => {
164   const n = Math.max(1, Math.min(count, 50));
165   const span = Math.max(1, range.max - range.min + 1);
166   // Deterministic fill: cycle through the range starting at `range.min`. This
167   // avoids `Math.random` so defaults stay stable (see note above) and is
168   // simple enough for users to understand at a glance. They can override any
169   // round value via the settings panel.
170   const fillValueAt = (index: number): number =>
171     range.min + (index % span);
172 
173   // Detect a collapsed state: every round has the same value even though the
174   // range allows variety. This happens when the user narrows the range down
175   // to a single value (collapsing every round to that value) and then widens
176   // it again — without this check the preserved values stay stuck at the
177   // collapse point and the player sees the same number every round. Also
178   // handles the empty-prev case from a legacy saved config.
179   const firstValue = prev[0]?.value;
180   const allCollapsed =
181     prev.length >= 2 &&
182     span > 1 &&
183     prev.every((r) => r.value === firstValue);
184   if (prev.length === 0 || allCollapsed) {
185     return Array.from({ length: n }, (_, i) => ({
186       value: fillValueAt(i),
187     }));
188   }
189 
190   const next = prev.slice(0, n).map((r, i) => {
191     if (r.value >= range.min && r.value <= range.max) return { ...r };
192     return { value: fillValueAt(i) };
193   });
194   while (next.length < n) {
195     next.push({ value: fillValueAt(next.length) });
196   }
197   return next;
198 };
199 
200 const makeDefaultSortNumbersConfig = (): SortNumbersConfig =>
201   resolveSimpleConfig({
202     configMode: 'simple',
203     direction: 'ascending',
204     start: 2,
205     step: 2,
206     quantity: 5,
207     distractors: false,
208   });
209 
210 export const resolveWordSpellConfig = (
211   saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
212 ): WordSpellConfig => {
213   const mode =
214     (saved as { mode?: unknown } | null)?.mode === 'picture'
215       ? 'picture'
216       : 'recall';
217   const base =
218     mode === 'picture' ? DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG : DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG;
219   const baseClone: WordSpellConfig = {
220     ...base,
221     rounds: base.rounds
222       ? base.rounds.map((r) => ({ ...r }))
223       : undefined,
224     source: base.source
225       ? { ...base.source, filter: { ...base.source.filter } }
226       : undefined,
227   };
228 
229   if (!saved || saved.component !== 'WordSpell') return baseClone;
230 
231   // Simple-mode: delegate to word-spell's library-source resolver.
232   if (saved.configMode === 'simple') {
233     const savedInput = saved.inputMethod;
234     return resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig({
235       configMode: 'simple',
236       ...saved,
237       inputMethod:
238         savedInput === 'type' || savedInput === 'both'
239           ? savedInput
240           : 'drag',
241     } as Parameters<typeof resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig>[0]);
242   }
243 
244   // Advanced merge — start from mode-appropriate base, then layer saved
245   // fields, then enforce the mode invariant.
246   const merged: WordSpellConfig = {
247     ...baseClone,
248     ...(saved as Partial<WordSpellConfig>),
249     gameId: 'word-spell',
250     component: 'WordSpell',
251   };
252 
253   // Mode invariant:
254   // recall  ⇒ source defined ∧ rounds undefined
255   // picture ⇒ rounds defined ∧ source undefined
256   if (merged.mode === 'recall') {
257     if (merged.rounds) {
258       const { rounds: _ignored, ...rest } = merged;
259       return rest as WordSpellConfig;
260     }
261     return merged;
262   }
263 
264   // mode === 'picture' (or 'sentence-gap' — treat as picture for default fallback)
265   const picture = { ...merged };
266   if (picture.source) {
267     delete picture.source;
268   }
269   if (!Array.isArray(picture.rounds) || picture.rounds.length === 0) {
270     picture.rounds = sliceWordSpellRounds(8);
271   }
272   picture.totalRounds = Math.min(
273     picture.rounds.length,
274     WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL.length,
275   );
276   return picture;
277 };
278 
279 const resolveNumberMatchConfig = (
280   saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
281 ): NumberMatchConfig => {
282   const base = makeDefaultNumberMatchConfig();
283   if (!saved || saved.component !== 'NumberMatch') return base;
284   const merged: NumberMatchConfig = {
285     ...base,
286     ...(saved as Partial<NumberMatchConfig>),
287     gameId: 'number-match',
288     component: 'NumberMatch',
289   };
```

 `./worktrees/feat-issue-216/src/games/word-spell/resolve-simple-config.ts`

1 import { defaultSelection, unitLevel } from './level-unit-selection';
2 import type { WordSpellConfig, WordSpellSimpleConfig } from './types';
3 import type {
4   GraphemePairFilter,
5   LevelGraphemeUnit,
6 } from '@/data/words';
7 import { GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL, cumulativeGraphemes } from '@/data/words';
8 
9 const uniqueGraphemePairs = (
10   units: readonly LevelGraphemeUnit[],
11 ): GraphemePairFilter[] => {
12   const seen = new Set<string>();
13   const out: GraphemePairFilter[] = [];
14   for (const u of units) {
15     const key = `${u.g}|${u.p}`;
16     if (seen.has(key)) continue;
17     seen.add(key);
18     out.push({ g: u.g, p: u.p });
19   }
20   return out;
21 };
22 
23 const maxLevelOf = (units: readonly LevelGraphemeUnit[]): number => {
24   if (units.length === 0) return 1;
25   let max = 1;
26   for (const u of units) {
27     const lvl = unitLevel(u);
28     if (lvl !== undefined && lvl > max) max = lvl;
29   }
30   return max;
31 };
32 
33 const unitsFromLegacy = (
34   raw: Record<string, unknown>,
35 ): LevelGraphemeUnit[] => {
36   if (
37     typeof raw.level !== 'number' ||
38     !Array.isArray(raw.phonemesAllowed)
39   )
40     return defaultSelection();
41   const out: LevelGraphemeUnit[] = [];
42   for (let lvl = 1; lvl <= raw.level; lvl++) {
43     for (const u of GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[lvl] ?? []) {
44       if ((raw.phonemesAllowed as string[]).includes(u.p)) out.push(u);
45     }
46   }
47   return out.length > 0 ? out : defaultSelection();
48 };
49 
50 export const resolveSimpleConfig = (
51   simple: WordSpellSimpleConfig,
52 ): WordSpellConfig => {
53   const raw = simple as unknown as Record<string, unknown>;
54   const units = Array.isArray(raw.selectedUnits)
55     ? (raw.selectedUnits as LevelGraphemeUnit[])
56     : unitsFromLegacy(raw);
57   const region =
58     typeof raw.region === 'string' ? (raw.region as 'aus') : 'aus';
59 
60   const maxLevel = maxLevelOf(units);
61   const graphemesAllowed = cumulativeGraphemes(maxLevel);
62   const graphemesRequired = uniqueGraphemePairs(units);
63 
64   return {
65     gameId: 'word-spell',
66     component: 'WordSpell',
67     configMode: 'simple',
68     mode: 'recall',
69     tileUnit: 'letter',
70     inputMethod: simple.inputMethod,
71     wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-manual',
72     ttsEnabled: true,
73     roundsInOrder: false,
74     totalRounds: 4,
75     tileBankMode: 'exact',
76     source: {
77       type: 'word-library',
78       filter: {
79         region,
80         graphemesAllowed,
81         graphemesRequired,
82       },
83     },
84   };
85 };
86 
87 export const advancedToSimple = (
88   config: WordSpellConfig,
89 ): WordSpellSimpleConfig => {
90   const filter = config.source?.filter;
91   const region = filter?.region ?? 'aus';
92 
93   if (
94     typeof filter?.level === 'number' &&
95     Array.isArray(filter.phonemesAllowed)
96   ) {
97     const out: LevelGraphemeUnit[] = [];
98     for (let lvl = 1; lvl <= filter.level; lvl++) {
99       for (const u of GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[lvl] ?? []) {
100         if (filter.phonemesAllowed.includes(u.p)) {
101           out.push(u);
102         }
103       }
104     }
105     return {
106       configMode: 'simple',
107       selectedUnits: out,
108       region,
109       inputMethod: config.inputMethod,
110     };
111   }
112 
113   return {
114     configMode: 'simple',
115     selectedUnits: defaultSelection(),
116     region,
117     inputMethod: config.inputMethod,
118   };
119 };
120
```

fix(word-spell): re-derive source.filter from selectedUnits on every resolve

Saved __last_session__ configs lacked configMode='simple', so
resolveWordSpellConfig fell into the advanced branch and never
re-derived source.filter from selectedUnits. Result: picker showed
L4 chips selected, but the persisted source.filter stayed at the
L1 default and the game produced L1 words.

Treat any config with selectedUnits as simple-mode and route through
resolveSimpleConfig so source.filter always reflects the picker.
Also migrate DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG to the post-fix graphemesRequired
shape — last phonemesRequired user removed from production code.

Regression test in resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-29 04:38:23Z)**_

All pre-push checks passed, pushed to `feat/issue-216`.

**Done.** Here's the summary:

**Fix:** `resolveWordSpellConfig` now treats any saved config with `selectedUnits` as simple-mode (line 234 in `$gameId.tsx`), routing it through `resolveSimpleConfig` which re-derives `source.filter` from the actual picker selection. Previously, configs without `configMode: 'simple'` fell into the advanced branch and kept the stale L1 default filter.

**Changes:**

- `$gameId.tsx:234` — condition broadened: `saved.configMode === 'simple' || Array.isArray(saved.selectedUnits)`
- `$gameId.tsx:114` — `DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG` migrated from `phonemesRequired` to `graphemesRequired` (last production user of the old shape)
- `resolveWordSpellConfig.test.ts` — regression test covering the exact saved-config shape from the IDB dump

**PR #219** will rebuild the preview at ~10 min. You'll want to verify manually: open WordSpell, untick L1, tick L4 chips only, start a round — words should be L4 (ship, chip, fish, etc.), zero L1 leakage.

**Open question from the handoff:** users with existing stale `__last_session__` in IDB will auto-heal now because the resolver re-derives on load. No clear-site-data needed — the fix is transparent.

1 import {
2   defaultSelection,
3   headerStateForLevel,
4   toggleLevel,
5   toggleUnit,
6   unitLevel,
7 } from '../level-unit-selection';
8 import { resolveSimpleConfig } from '../resolve-simple-config';

10 import type {
11   LevelGraphemeUnit,
12   Region,
13   WordFilter,
14 } from '@/data/words';
15 import type { JSX } from 'react';
16 import { GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL } from '@/data/words';
17 
18 type Props = {
19   config: Record<string, unknown>;
20   onChange: (config: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
21 };
22 
23 const LEVELS = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8] as const;
24 
25 const readSelected = (
26   config: Record<string, unknown>,
27 ): LevelGraphemeUnit[] => {
28   const raw = config.selectedUnits;
29   return Array.isArray(raw)
30     ? (raw as LevelGraphemeUnit[])
31     : defaultSelection();
32 };
33 
34 const readRegion = (config: Record<string, unknown>): Region =>
35   typeof config.region === 'string' ? (config.region as Region) : 'aus';
36 
37 const maxLevelOf = (units: readonly LevelGraphemeUnit[]): number => {
38   if (units.length === 0) return 1;
39   let max = 1;
40   for (const u of units) {
41     const lvl = unitLevel(u);
42     if (lvl !== undefined && lvl > max) max = lvl;
43   }
44   return max;
45 };
46 
47 const chipsForLevel = (
48   level: number,
49 ): { value: string; label: string; units: LevelGraphemeUnit[] }[] => {
50   const byPhoneme = new Map<string, LevelGraphemeUnit[]>();
51   for (const u of GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[level] ?? []) {
52     if (u.p === '') continue;
53     const existing = byPhoneme.get(u.p);
54     if (existing) existing.push(u);
55     else byPhoneme.set(u.p, [u]);
56   }
57   return [...byPhoneme.entries()].map(([p, units]) => ({
58     value: `${level}|${p}`,
59     label: `${units.map((u) => u.g).join(', ')} /${p}/`,
60     units,
61   }));
62 };
63 
64 const headerAriaPressed = (
65   state: ReturnType<typeof headerStateForLevel>,
66 ): 'true' | 'false' | 'mixed' => {
67   if (state.kind === 'all-on') return 'true';
68   if (state.kind === 'partial') return 'mixed';
69   return 'false';
70 };
71 
72 const headerSubtitle = (
73   state: ReturnType<typeof headerStateForLevel>,
74 ): string => {
75   switch (state.kind) {
76     case 'all-on': {
77       return `${state.count} / ${state.total} sounds`;
78     }
79     case 'partial': {
80       return `partial ${state.count} / ${state.total} sounds`;
81     }
82     case 'tiles-only': {
83       return 'tiles only';
84     }
85     case 'not-in-scope': {
86       return 'not in scope';
87     }
88   }
89 };
90 
91 const headerBgClass = (
92   state: ReturnType<typeof headerStateForLevel>,
93 ): string => {
94   switch (state.kind) {
95     case 'all-on': {
96       return 'bg-primary text-primary-foreground';
97     }
98     case 'partial': {
99       return 'bg-primary/40 text-foreground';
100     }
101     case 'tiles-only': {
102       return 'bg-muted text-foreground';
103     }
104     case 'not-in-scope': {
105       return 'bg-muted/40 text-muted-foreground';
106     }
107   }
108 };
109 
110 const LevelRow = ({
111   level,
112   selected,
113   maxLevel,
114   onChange,
115 }: {
116   level: number;
117   selected: LevelGraphemeUnit[];
118   maxLevel: number;
119   onChange: (next: LevelGraphemeUnit[]) => void;
120 }): JSX.Element => {
121   const state = headerStateForLevel(level, selected, maxLevel);
122   const chips = chipsForLevel(level);
123 
124   const isUnitOn = (u: LevelGraphemeUnit): boolean =>
125     selected.some((s) => s.g === u.g && s.p === u.p);
126 
127   const handleHeader = () => {
128     onChange(
129       toggleLevel(
130         level,
131         selected,
132         state.kind === 'all-on' ? 'unchecked' : 'checked',
133       ),
134     );
135   };
136 
137   const handleChip = (units: LevelGraphemeUnit[]) => {
138     let acc = selected;
139     for (const u of units) acc = toggleUnit(u, acc);
140     onChange(acc);
141   };
142 
143   return (
144     <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
145       <button
146         type="button"
147         onClick={handleHeader}
148         aria-pressed={headerAriaPressed(state)}
149         className={`flex items-center justify-between rounded-md px-3 py-2 text-sm font-semibold ${headerBgClass(state)}`}
150       >
151         <span>Level {level}</span>
152         <span className="text-xs font-normal">
153           {headerSubtitle(state)}
154         </span>
155       </button>
156 
157       <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1">
158         {chips.map((c) => {
159           const on = c.units.every((u) => isUnitOn(u));
160           const base = 'rounded-full px-2.5 py-1 text-xs font-bold';
161           const cls = on
162             ? `${base} bg-primary text-primary-foreground`
163             : `${base} border border-border bg-muted text-foreground`;
164           return (
165             <button
166               key={c.value}
167               type="button"
168               onClick={() => handleChip(c.units)}
169               aria-pressed={on}
170               aria-label={c.label}
171               className={cls}
172             >
173               {c.label}
174             </button>
175           );
176         })}
177       </div>
178     </div>
179   );
180 };
181 
182 const buildPreviewFilter = (
183   selected: LevelGraphemeUnit[],
184   region: Region,
185 ): WordFilter => {
186   const resolved = resolveSimpleConfig({
187     configMode: 'simple',
188     selectedUnits: selected,
189     region,
190     inputMethod: 'drag',
191   });
192   return resolved.source!.filter;
193 };
194 
195 export const WordSpellLibrarySource = ({
196   config,
197   onChange,
198 }: Props): JSX.Element => {
199   const selected = readSelected(config);
200   const region = readRegion(config);
201   const invalid = selected.length === 0;
202   const maxLevel = maxLevelOf(selected);
203 
204   const setSelected = (next: LevelGraphemeUnit[]) => {
205     onChange({ ...config, selectedUnits: next });
206   };
207 
208   return (
209     <div
210       className="flex flex-col gap-4"
211       data-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : 'false'}
212     >
213       {LEVELS.map((n) => (
214         <LevelRow
215           key={n}
216           level={n}
217           selected={selected}
218           maxLevel={maxLevel}
219           onChange={setSelected}
220         />
221       ))}
222       <WordPreviewBar filter={buildPreviewFilter(selected, region)} />
223       {invalid && (
224         <p className="mt-2 text-xs text-destructive">
225           Pick at least one sound to play.
226         </p>
227       )}
228     </div>
229   );
230 };
231

fix(word-spell): preserve selectedUnits round-trip and respect advanced mode

The previous fix routed all configs with selectedUnits through
resolveSimpleConfig, which (a) stripped selectedUnits from the output
(breaking the level-picker round-trip — chips snapped to L1 on every
change) and (b) ignored configMode='advanced' (resetting totalRounds
to 4 and losing advanced settings).

Three fixes:
1. Add selectedUnits to WordSpellConfig and include it in
   resolveSimpleConfig output so the picker can read it back.
2. Guard the simple-mode condition: only auto-detect simple when
   configMode is not explicitly 'advanced'.
3. In the advanced branch, re-derive source.filter from selectedUnits
   when present — the picker updates selectedUnits but not the filter,
   so the filter was stale in advanced mode too.

Regression tests for both the no-configMode (IDB) case and the
advanced-mode-with-selectedUnits case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-29 04:56:00Z)**_

All pre-push checks passed, pushed.

Here's what I fixed:

**Bug 1 (can't select/unselect levels in simple mode):** `resolveSimpleConfig` was stripping `selectedUnits` from its output. The picker reads `config.selectedUnits` to render chip states — when it was gone, every re-render fell back to `defaultSelection()` (L1). Fix: added `selectedUnits` to `WordSpellConfig` type and included it in `resolveSimpleConfig`'s return value.

**Bug 2 (advanced mode reverting to L1/4 rounds):** My original condition `Array.isArray(saved.selectedUnits)` ignored `configMode: 'advanced'` (set explicitly by the advanced modal at line 124). Everything got routed through `resolveSimpleConfig` which hardcodes `totalRounds: 4`. Fix: the condition now checks `saved.configMode !== 'advanced'` before the `selectedUnits` fallback. Additionally, the advanced branch now re-derives `source.filter` from `selectedUnits` when present — the picker updates `selectedUnits` but never touched the filter, so it was stale in advanced mode too.

The PR preview will rebuild in ~10 min. Test on your iPhone: tap level chips in simple mode (should toggle), switch to advanced, set 8 rounds, save, reload — settings should persist.

---
